grader.method: Independent 17 July 2026 course-wide semantic regrade of all 187 composed pages after concept-first visual headings, cumulative story reveals, outcome-heading removal, shared lab contrast rules, and retirement of 21 supplemental program bridges; confirmed that required transfer, depth, checks, boundaries, and provenance remain intact.

pages.rationale: The home makes grounding, closed-loop control, and system-level proof a compelling six-part perception-language-action journey, with two direct CTAs and a simulation-qualified finish line. Requested versus applied action and physical-evidence boundaries remain clear. E is 14 because the earlier coordinate trace is no longer rendered; R is 4 because this home has no claim-linked source cards.

The page introduces embodied AI through a mug-picking sense–decide–act–check loop and explains how sensors, movement, timing, feedback, and physical hazards change the task. The located-mug, unconfirmed-grasp, and person-in-path lab makes continue, retry, and stop decisions concrete; committed checks, changed-case transfer, deterministic code, and quiz remain prerequisite-free. The official robotics source is useful but broad rather than pinned to the exact introductory claims.

Shapes, dtypes, units, frames, timestamps, masks, requested/applied commands, and saturation are introduced before use and remain consistent across the 90-degree tool-frame and 7-cm-request/3-cm-applied traces. Prediction-gated code, lab cases, and a changed-robot transfer check fully test both outcomes. The only deduction is broad page-level official documentation rather than a versioned schema section.

Transform direction, composition, inversion, and the .20 to .70 to 1.70 to .20 round trip are correct, while 1 m/s times 120 ms correctly yields .12 m of temporal displacement. The page activates typed-space prerequisites and uses code, lab, objective retrieval, and transfer to expose frame and clock mistakes. Its supporting book link is a landing page rather than a chapter or equation locator.

The page explicitly declares the rigid-grasp world-frame assumption, advances .40 by three applied .02-m steps to .46, grows variance from .001 to .0022, and invalidates the trace on grasp break, frame mismatch, or stale belief. The learner must predict, inspect three belief cases, and transfer to a changed observation history. Provenance remains broad rather than claim-local.

The complete capstone builds reset/step, versioned schemas, frames, clocks, predicates, requested/applied logs, replay state, and fault containment in staged learner work. The dependency-free starter executed with every check true, the nominal-versus-80-ms-stale artifact preserves raw rows and honest fixture boundaries, and the page supplies expected results, gates, hints, rubric, reference package, reflections, and project-local primary sources.

The 18/4/7/61-ms packet example correctly passes RGB, joints, and force while rejecting stale depth; ADC-rail saturation, calibration revision, validity, common-clock assumptions, and requested diagnostic actions are explicit. Objective checks, runnable fixture, sensor-failure lab, transfer, and quiz all expose changed sensor evidence. The source cards are current and direct but not pinned to the exact sensing claims.

The page separates fit from held-out calibration evidence, applies the .004-versus-.006 release and .011 invalidation gates correctly, and computes root-sum-square uncertainty from .006 and .008 as .010 under stated independence assumptions. Learners predict, run the arithmetic, inspect drift cases, and transfer to changed calibration evidence. The supporting reference lacks a claim-local chapter or equation pin.

The .06-m center distance correctly fails the .05 point rule and passes the explicit .05+.04=.09 extent-aware rule. Object identity, permutation invariance, unknown-space risk, geometry, uncertainty, and downstream predicates are taught through exact checks, executable code, three lab cases, and changed-case transfer. Source support remains page-level rather than attached to each representation claim.

The repaired page now defines observation matrix H before relying on y=z-H x-hat, fixes H=1 in the scalar worked trace, and defines negative log likelihood as -log p(z), including its lower-is-better interpretation and its dependence on a suitable probability model. The recomputed trace still reaches .40/.04, innovations .10/.50, gain .8, and posterior .48/.008, while identity-switch transfer prevents low RMSE from hiding the wrong object. The only deduction is that the supporting book source remains broad rather than claim-local.

The capstone stages packet validation, transforms, prediction/update, covariance, identity, evaluator-only truth, abstention, replay, and delay recovery as one build. Its starter ran with all checks true, the [.07,.04,.018,.012] recovery trace correctly identifies the third packet under a .02 gate, and the reference artifact, matrix, failure evidence, rubric, retries, and reviewed project-local sources make the implementation and limits inspectable.

Joystick .8 times .05 m correctly requests .04 m, the limiter applies .03 m, and the applied command owns the next transition while intent, timing, operator, intervention, and rights provenance remain logged. Operator-mixture confounding, failure retention, prediction gates, and changed-case transfer are all effective. The DAgger and robotics links are relevant but not claim-local teleoperation protocol citations.

Episode/step lineage, applied-action ownership, checksums, endings, embodiment/calibration revisions, rights, and split-before-windowing are coherent. The E7/E8 example and the constructed 98%-to-74% leakage contrast make the generalization claim observable, while code, lab, and new-robot transfer enforce source-group isolation. Paper links are direct but remain paper-level rather than claim-pinned.

The page correctly computes 12/10,000=.0012=.12%, separates total volume from critical-slice coverage, and treats duplication, leakage, consent, provenance, and missing failures as independent release gates. Learners predict the audit result, run the calculation, compare three data dossiers, and transfer to a changed mixture. Governance source attribution is relevant but broad rather than claim-linked.

H=4 at 20 Hz correctly spans .20 s, an episode with two steps left uses mask [1,1,0,0], and executing two actions replans after .10 s; the latency, smoothness, and recovery activities remain strong. The repaired boundary and vocabulary now state explicitly that predicting several future actions or increasing H does not create multimodality: the decoder and training objective must represent distinct plausible sequences. The only deduction is that the direct sources remain paper-level rather than claim-local.

The complete project specifies episode splits, typed masked targets, scaling, one-batch fit, checkpoints, deterministic resume, requested/applied logs, support departure, and paired nominal/changed-start rollouts. Its starter executed all four checks, the zero-based step-2 support exit and step-5 workspace exit are coherent, and the full artifact, failure log, hard gates, rubric, worked reference, and DAgger boundary support both build and experiment outcomes.

The semantic-.9/affordance-0 candidate and semantic-.4/affordance-.8 candidate correctly yield abstention because neither satisfies all predicates. Referents, relations, goal predicates, feasibility, clarification, counterfactual grounding, and changed discourse are exercised through objective checks, runnable gates, lab, transfer, and quiz. SayCan and RT-1 are primary and relevant but linked at paper rather than exact-claim level.

The 64+12+1+1 token ledger correctly gives [B,78,128], full attention scores [B,H,78,78], and 77 valid keys after masked proprioception loss. The page distinguishes width alignment from semantic grounding, then uses missing-modality and target/background counterfactuals to test reliance. RT-1 and Open X-Embodiment are direct sources, but their cards remain paper-level rather than section-pinned.

For [instruction,o0,a0,o1,a1], placing the a1 predictor at o1 correctly excludes the target while allowing the earlier applied a0; [2,5,32], [2,1,5,5], and [2,5,4] shapes are consistent. The explicitly labeled pseudocode, future-token invariance lab, 95%-teacher-forced/40%-rollout gap, transfer, and retries diagnose leakage and exposure bias. ACT and RT-1 sources are broad paper links.

With alpha=.8, sigma=.6, and noise [.5,.5], corruption correctly produces [1.1,-.5], and predicted noise [.4,.2] gives the stated [1.075,-.775] estimate without changing action shape. The page separates denoising computation from environment actions and tests the 20-step/120-ms versus 50-ms deadline trade-off plus stale conditioning. The primary Diffusion Policy link is relevant but not section-pinned.

The capstone joins grounded bindings, multimodal tokens and masks, typed chunks, causal transformer and diffusion decoders, normalization, timing, support, constraints, counterfactuals, and closed-loop evaluation under matched evidence. Its deterministic starter correctly rejects both the 60-ms diffusion arm and the ungrounded transformer arm, while the staged build, expected checks, full artifact, rubric, failures, reference package, and reviewed project-local papers make the non-winner decision reproducible.

The proportional example computes error .4, requested command .8, and applied saturated command .5, then correctly separates windup, delay, sign, stability, and independent safety authority. Prediction-led code, growing-oscillation lab, residual-controller transfer, and quiz test control consequences rather than vocabulary recall. The sole Gymnasium-Robotics link is current but does not directly pin the feedback and anti-windup claims.

The declared mean-minus-uncertainty fixture scores A=7 and B=6, selects A subject to constraints, and replans after a prefix; predicted-versus-real rank reversal correctly identifies model exploitation. Matched-call transfer, candidate logging, fallback cases, code, and quiz fully support both outcomes and their simulator boundary. The robotics and SayCan sources are relevant but not pinned to the planner claims.

The grasp(cup) trace correctly requires reachable and gripper-empty, refuses to assert holding after timeout without contact, and routes to reobserve. Typed preconditions, arguments, termination, postconditions, failure taxonomy, recovery edges, and cached-success transfer are taught through two objective checks, state-machine code, three diagnostic cases, and quiz. SayCan and RT-1 support the area but remain paper-level citations.

Under the explicit frictionless model, 2 N divided by 1 m/s^2 correctly identifies 2 kg, while unknown friction makes one equation insufficient to identify both terms. The [.2,.8] randomization range versus 1.2 test is correctly labeled extrapolation, and transfer prevents final-test retuning from remaining a test. The foundational domain-randomization source is direct but not claim-local.

The full project places constraint and emergency authority after optimizing components, preserves synchronized state and requested/applied actions, injects one fault, and distinguishes autonomous from human-assisted recovery. Its starter ran all checks, the 20-ms detection/three-packet/human-resume trace is labeled assisted rather than autonomous, and the artifact, stop/recovery gates, common-mode failure, rubric, reflections, and project-local sources satisfy both build and experiment promises.

The 9/10 and 2/10 cell rates, approximate Wilson intervals [.596,.982] and [.057,.510], and equal-cell macro .55 were recomputed and are correct. The page defines the independent episode unit, prevents correlated frames from becoming n=1,000, separates assisted outcomes and hard gates, and uses code, lab, transfer, and retries diagnostically. LIBERO and robotics sources are direct but remain broad.

The page correctly distinguishes task-preserving texture perturbation from a causal red-to-blue instruction change, identifies 4 cm as the first success-below-.8 severity, and separately diagnoses .95 confidence as shifted-slice miscalibration. Paired counterfactual lab cases, deterministic onset code, held-out-lineage transfer, and boundary language fully teach the claims. LIBERO and domain-randomization links are relevant but paper-level.

The page correctly labels p50=32 ms as a typical-value pass but p99=88 ms as a 38-ms violation of the declared p99<=50-ms SLO, then replaces the late request with hold. Stage timing, watchdog containment, incident provenance, schema-compatible rollback, residual risk, changed-case transfer, and executable fixture all agree. The official robotics source is current but does not directly pin the SLO and operations claims.

The page supplies pinned Python/Torch/NumPy setup, portable Colab/local commands, exact smoke and five-seed full budgets, expected invariant fields, variable observations, stop rules, troubleshooting, completion criteria, and a no-promised-winner evidence boundary. The repaired runner separately checks every recorded training loss, recursively validates every numeric leaf in training and evaluation summaries, and therefore covers nested requested/applied actions, episode positions, disturbances, errors, and aggregate values; injected non-finite values in each category correctly failed the invariant. The local smoke-gate notebook and external contract checks pass, so the visible executable contract is now fully supported.

The final capstone requires baseline acceptance before one matched intervention, declares independent seeds/cells and primitive budgets, preserves assistance and failures, applies grounding/constraint/deadline gates, and treats null or negative results as complete. Its starter executed all checks, the .85-mean treatment correctly fails a zero-deadline-miss gate, and the live four-stage workspace, validation terms, rubric, complete reference artifact, reflections, and reviewed project-local sources make the original study reproducible and bounded.
